regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, expect east winds at 8 knots with a 75% rain probability accompanied by drizzle, which is expected to impact port operations and shore excursions. Port Everglades will experience similar conditions with east winds near 8 knots and a 67% rain probability tomorrow, also including drizzle likely to affect embarkation and onshore activities. Port Canaveral should prepare for east winds at 8 knots and a 64% rain probability with drizzle on the third day, maintaining a wet environment for cruise operations. Mariners and passengers should be ready for persistent light rain and reduced visibility over the next 72 hours as these conditions gradually ease but remain steady.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, expect east winds at 15 knots with a 57% rain probability and drizzle in Nassau, which is expected to impact port operations and may affect shore excursions in Freeport and Bimini as well. Visibility will remain adequate despite the drizzle under mostly cloudy skies. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift slightly southeast at 14 knots with rain probabilities dropping to 41% on day two, potentially affecting embarkation activities in Nassau, Key West, and Grand Cayman with light drizzle continuing. By day three, east-southeast winds stay steady at 14 knots with a 45% chance of drizzle, which may affect operations in Ocho Rios and Falmouth. Passengers and crew should prepare for intermittent light rain and plan accordingly for outdoor activities during these periods.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, an elevated rain chance of 68% with drizzle is expected to impact port operations and shore excursions in San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Maarten as easterly winds increase to 19 knots. Beyond 48 hours, drizzle will persist with a 29% rain probability and steady east winds at 17 knots, maintaining cautious conditions for all eastern Caribbean ports. Crew and passengers should prepare for damp conditions and moderate winds through midweek.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, Cozumel will see east-southeast winds at 8 knots with a 52% rain probability accompanied by light drizzle, which may affect shore excursions and port operations. regions.southern-caribbean: Today, cruise operations in Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena will experience elevated east winds around 21 knots, accompanied by overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting steady, albeit breezy, conditions for embarkation and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect sustained east winds at 21 knots with continued overcast skies and minimal rain chances, maintaining consistent conditions across all Southern Caribbean ports. Beyond 48 hours, the forecast remains stable with persistent east winds at 21 knots and overcast skies, so cruise itineraries should plan for ongoing elevated wind impacts but low likelihood of precipitation disruptions. Passengers and crew should prepare for steady breezes that could influence smaller craft handling and outdoor activities while rain remains an unlikely factor throughout the region.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today, expect easterly winds at 14 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions in Barbados without rain interruptions. regions.los-angeles: Today, conditions at Los Angeles and San Pedro ports show light winds from the west-southwest at 2 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation operations as well as shore excursions around Catalina Island and Marina del Rey.
